instinct_candidates.title: Prefer basedpyright over LSP diagnostics for Python gates
instinct_candidates.trigger: When you need to assess/resolve Python type issues in a repo that treats basedpyright as a required gate (or explicitly instructs to use it).
instinct_candidates.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` and fix reported issues; do not rely on editor/LSP diagnostics or the `lsp_diagnostics` tool as a substitute for the gate.

instinct_candidates.title: Compound autolearn responses are JSON-only and tool-free
instinct_candidates.trigger: When acting as the background autolearn/compound learning agent producing memory-only updates.
instinct_candidates.action: Do not run any tools; output exactly one valid JSON object (no markdown). If there is no durable learning worth persisting, output `{}`. Keep within budgets and avoid proposing product code changes.

skill_candidates.name: compound-autolearn-proposal
skill_candidates.description: Generate durable, memory-only compound updates from a session summary while respecting strict JSON/tooling and budget constraints.
skill_candidates.body:
# Skill: Compound Autolearn Proposal

## Purpose
Propose memory-only updates (instincts/skills) from recent activity without touching product code.

## Hard Constraints
- Do not run tools.
- Output a single JSON object only (no markdown/fences).
- If nothing is durable, output `{}`.
- Stay within the budget caps provided by the prompt.
- Prefer updating an existing skill/instinct (same intent) over creating a near-duplicate.

## Procedure
1. Scan the provided context for durable patterns:
   - Repeated mistakes or near-misses (quality gates, safety rules, UX constraints).
   - Repo-specific invariants (required commands, required order of checks).
   - Clear IO contracts ("output must be JSON only", "no tools", etc.).
2. Filter aggressively:
   - Skip one-off fixes and situational details.
   - Skip anything already explicitly stated in the prompt unless it is frequently violated.
3. Draft instinct candidates (max 8):
   - `id`: kebab-case, stable, specific.
   - `trigger`: concrete and testable (when to apply).
   - `action`: short, imperative, deterministic.
   - `confidence`: 0..1 reflecting evidence strength.
   - `tags`: small set (3-6) for retrieval.
4. Draft skill candidates (max 3) only if procedural guidance will be reused:
   - Keep short and step-based.
   - Reference repo-root-relative paths when needed.
   - Include `source_instinct_ids` tying back to relevant instincts.
5. Emit the final JSON:
   - Include only `instinct_candidates` and/or `skill_candidates`.
   - Ensure strict JSON validity (double quotes, no trailing commas).

## Quality Bar
Only propose updates that would still be valuable weeks later and reduce repeated failures or confusion.
